frames.name: Electoral Horse Race
frames.description: Focuses on polling numbers, campaign spending, and electoral mechanics like runoff possibilities
frames.emphasis: Who's winning, spending amounts ($122m), polling data, and procedural requirements like 50% threshold for avoiding runoffs

frames.name: Ideological Battle Within Parties
frames.description: Presents primaries as contests between different political philosophies and approaches
frames.emphasis: MAGA vs. establishment Republicans; progressive 'resistance' vs. bridge-building Democrats

frames.name: National Implications
frames.description: Views Texas races as indicators for broader midterm trends and party control
frames.emphasis: How results will affect House/Senate control, resource allocation for other battleground states

frames.name: Historical Context
frames.description: Emphasizes the rarity of Democratic statewide victories in Texas
frames.emphasis: Democrats haven't won statewide since 1994, potential for historic change

frames.name: Candidate Contrast
frames.description: Highlights stark differences in candidate styles and backgrounds
frames.emphasis: Paxton's scandals vs. Cornyn's establishment credentials; Crockett's 'firebrand' style vs. Talarico's 'politics of love'

outlierPerspectives: Talarico's unique framing of politics as 'top vs. bottom' rather than 'left vs. right' and his use of Christian faith to justify progressive policies
The specific mention of Trump's role in mid-decade gerrymandering to preserve GOP House majority
Focus on early voting surge, particularly on Democratic side, as indicator of engagement
missingContext: Specific policy positions of the candidates beyond general ideological descriptions
Detailed explanation of the scandals surrounding Ken Paxton beyond brief mentions
Analysis of demographic changes in Texas that might affect electoral viability
Historical performance data for similar primary matchups
Explanation of why North Carolina and Arkansas primaries receive minimal coverage despite being mentioned
Details about the congressional races also occurring, which are only briefly mentioned
Voter turnout data or expectations beyond the general 'surge' reference
